title: Legends of Expedition: The Crumbling Pyramid
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=aMG8tqZ6bW0

Daniel (@dungeondive) explores the VHS-box-sized Legends of Expedition: The Crumbling Pyramid from Upsize Fun, a dice manipulation game dripping with pulp adventure vibes. You're a team of adventurers trapped inside a collapsing ancient pyramid, racing to decipher clues and escape before the whole structure comes down around your ears—with a mummy in hot pursuit, naturally.

The gameplay involves rolling your party's dice pool against challenge dice, matching values to overcome obstacles while managing resources like supplies, letters from home, and Egyptology notes. There's a delightful danger card mechanic where entering perilous rooms might spawn anything from scarabs to alligators. Daniel particularly appreciates that Gameccrafter used pre-designed chit sheets, sparing players the tedium of wiping laser-cut soot off their tokens.

The rulebook could use some polish (there's at least one error regarding tool usage), but the game delivers a satisfying blend of dice rolling, light resource management, and thematic adventure without demanding excessive table space. For those seeking that Indiana Jones energy in a compact package, this pyramid awaits your exploration.

----

*Would you brave the crumbling corridors, or does dice-driven doom sound like too perilous an expedition?*
